scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a facilities-specific availability summary grounded in work-order scope, vendor ETA, permit posture, outage timing, and return-to-service prerequisites.
Missing permit, access, and shutdown artifacts were treated as blockers and not reinterpreted as implicit approval to dispatch the vendor.
Shortage posture was preserved with downtime impact and no unsupported claim that the part inventory could cover all facilities.
Vendor delay was translated into a bounded vendor-risk note with outage and restoration consequences, not an over-authoritative operational decision.
The lane refused to bypass spend approval and vendor-release controls and routed the request to the proper approval owner.
A code-sensitive substitution and repair-method request was escalated to design and licensed-trade owners instead of being approved as routine vendor management.
Incomplete inventory and installed-base evidence blocked the recommendation until authoritative traceability could be established.
Open inspection deficiencies and missing closeout artifacts prevented the lane from representing vendor completion as return-to-service completion.
Safety observations and inspection hold points changed the answer and correctly triggered escalation rather than a confident dispatch or restoration recommendation.
The lane rejected licensed-trade, design, and code work and returned boundary-safe routing guidance.
Missing jurisdiction, CMMS, vendor-control, and release criteria facts correctly triggered a low-confidence blocked note instead of fabricated specificity.
Conflicting public guidance and tenant SOP were escalated for human resolution instead of being synthesized into a definitive restoration rule.
Facilities terminology was normalized into authoritative records and assumptions while preserving the public baseline and tenant overlay boundaries.

notes: All 13 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to facilities vendor management, maintenance-state evidence, permit and inspection gating, outage and restoration boundaries, and traceable supply status.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all supply-procurement-and-availability abilities, and all construction-and-field-services abilities required by the issue contract.
